title: Community notice board rules
summary: A public board format for verified local information without exposing private details.
content: A notice board reduces repeated questions and phone battery use. Use sections: verified updates, water, food, health services, missing information requests, volunteer tasks, and next meeting time. Every note needs date, time, source, and whether it is confirmed. Remove old notes or mark them old. Keep private medical or security details off the board.
materials: Clean storage space. Context: “Community notice board rules”.
steps: Require date, time, source, and confirmation status.
warnings: Do not publish exact locations of vulnerable people publicly.
Do not post rumors as facts.
common_mistakes: Treating Community notice board rules as finished after the first attempt instead of writing the next review time.
Moving people or supplies for Community notice board rules without checking vulnerable people, exits, and safe limits first.
Mixing clean and unsafe items during Community notice board rules because labels, dates, or responsible names were not written down.
alternatives: Run a smaller version of Community notice board rules with one responsible person, one helper, and one visible paper note.
Use a safer fallback location or method for Community notice board rules if the first choice becomes wet, crowded, contaminated, dark, unstable, or unsafe.
Pause Community notice board rules and verify the written record with a second person before moving scarce supplies.
failure_signs: People cannot explain who is responsible for Community notice board rules or when the next check happens.
Supplies connected to Community notice board rules are unlabelled, damp, contaminated, damaged, missing, or being used for the wrong purpose.
The routine ignores clear records, verified messages, offline copies, and review times and starts creating new risk instead of reducing existing risk during “Community notice board rules”
when_not_to_use: Do not use Community notice board rules as a reason to delay evacuation, emergency calls, medical care, fire response, or official local instructions.
Do not use Community notice board rules when the location is structurally unsafe, chemically suspicious, smoky, flooded, violent, or otherwise immediately dangerous.
Do not improvise hazardous repairs, dosing, chemical handling, weapon use, or electrical/gas work under the label of Community notice board rules.
short_term: For the first hours of Community notice board rules, keep the action small, visible, and reversible: write the goal, assign one owner, protect vulnerable people, separate safe from unsafe supplies, and set a review time.
long_term: If Community notice board rules remains necessary for days or weeks, rotate roles, preserve written lessons, replace fragile supplies, and train another person so the group does not depend on one expert.
if_method_fails: If Community notice board rules fails, stop escalation, move to the safest fallback, record what failed, protect people first, and restart only with a simpler written plan.
environment_notes: Adapt Community notice board rules to the real environment: check clear records, verified messages, offline copies, and review times, local weather, building condition, water access, light, noise, animals, neighbours, and safe exits before repeating the routine.
